title: # Start Kernel Orchestration Start Kernel orchestration for the current task inside this Codex session. Execute immediately. Do not create or edit GitHub issues, pull requests, review comments, or issue comments. Do not inspect CI unless explicitly asked later. Backup-only GitHub Actions dispatch or...
summary_text: Yes, your diagnosis is correct and this is the right minimal direction. Current behavior in [session_watch.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-kernel-guard/src/codex_kernel_guard/session_watch.py#L615) to [session_watch.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-kernel-guard/src/codex_kernel_guard/session_watch.py#L707) spends `startup_grace_seconds` while resolving rollout, then passes `remaining_startup` (often `0`) into `watch_session_jsonl`, so lane ramp has effectively no startup grace and can fail after only `low_lane_grace_seconds` (5s). For **smoke convergence**, the minimal safe fix is: - Keep rollout-resolution loop as-is. - After rollout is found, pass a fresh startup grace to watcher for smoke/quorum runs (where `CODEX_KERNEL_SUCCESS_ON_QUORUM=1`), instead of `remaining_startup`. This keeps behavior tight for normal runs, while fixing the smoke-specific early fail-close. Regression risks to note: 1. If applied unconditionally, total tolerated startup delay increases (resolution grace + watcher grace), so true startup failures fail later. 2. Longer grace can mask rollout-resolution slowness or DB jitter symptoms. 3. If lane spawning is actually broken, process runtime before fail-close increases. So: **resetting startup grace is the correct minimal fix**, but best scoped to smoke/quorum path to avoid broad behavior drift.
